Definition: Spinus spinus genome assembly, chromosome: 6
Description: Spinus spinus genome assembly, chromosome: 6
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Passeriformes; Passeroidea; Fringillidae; Carduelinae; Spinus
Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly bSpiSpi1.1 of the Eurasian siskin, Spinus spinus. The sample was provided by Jan T. Lifjeld, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bSpiSpi1.1 is based on 32x PacBio HiFi data and 69x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Definition: Sparganium emersum genome assembly, organelle: mitochondrion
Description: Sparganium emersum genome assembly, organelle: mitochondrion
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Tracheophyta; Spermatophyta; Magnoliopsida; Liliopsida; Poales; Typhaceae; Sparganium
Assembly: The assembly lpSpaEmer1.hap1.1 is based on 52x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome contains a number of heterozygous inversions on the following chromosomes: chromosome 5 (23.4-24.2Mbp) and chromosome 6 (16.9-18.8Mbp). There is also a heterozygous translocation on chromosome 10 (13.5-13.8 Mbp).

Definition: Harmothoe areolata genome assembly, chromosome: 13
Description: Harmothoe areolata genome assembly, chromosome: 13
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Annelida; Polychaeta; Errantia; Phyllodocida; Polynoidae; Harmothoe
Assembly: The assembly of Harmothoe areolata (wpHarAreo1.1) is based on 41X PacBio data and Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 853 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 40.89 Mb (with the largest being 1.79 Mb). Additionally, 591 regions totaling 125.49 Mb (with the largest being 6.41 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 42 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 72.56 Mb (with the largest being 6.43 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Definition: Sparganium erectum genome assembly, organelle: mitochondrion
Description: Sparganium erectum genome assembly, organelle: mitochondrion
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Tracheophyta; Spermatophyta; Magnoliopsida; Liliopsida; Poales; Typhaceae; Sparganium
Assembly: The assembly lpSpaErec1.hap1.1 is based on 139x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.
